description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) Schmid factor distribution maps illustrating the deformation behavior of a duplex lightweight steel (Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni) under increasing tensile strains of 0%, 1%, 3%, and 6%. The upper row (a1-d1) displays the Schmid factor evolution for the austenite (γ) phase, while the lower row (a2-d2) shows the B2 intermetallic phase, with respective color scales ranging up to 0.5 and 0.2. Each micrograph includes a 5 μm scale bar. The maps reveal heterogeneous deformation across individual grains, with color variations indicating different crystallographic orientations and slip system activities. As strain increases, the Schmid factor distributions evolve distinctly between the two phases, reflecting strain partitioning and coordinated deformation. These observations support the analysis of geometrically necessary dislocation (GND) accumulation at austenite/B2 interfaces, which contributes to hetero-deformation induced (HDI) strengthening, as the caption and citation context indicate that Schmid factor variations explain differences in GND densities among grains.
